quiz.title: SAT Mathematics: Unit 10 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Lines and Angles
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation
quiz.topic: Angle relationships, parallel lines, and transversals

questions.question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. If one of the alternate interior angles measures $(3x + 10)^\circ$ and its corresponding alternate interior angle measures $(x + 50)^\circ$, what is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate interior angles are equal. Set the expressions equal: $3x + 10 = x + 50$. Subtract $x$ from both sides: $2x + 10 = 50$. Subtract 10: $2x = 40$, so $x = 20$.

questions.question: Lines $l$ and $m$ are parallel. A transversal $t$ intersects both lines. If a pair of same-side interior angles are represented by $(2x + 20)^\circ$ and $(3x + 10)^\circ$, what is the measure of the larger angle?
questions.explanation: Same-side interior angles are supplementary (sum to $180^\circ$). $(2x + 20) + (3x + 10) = 180 \Rightarrow 5x + 30 = 180 \Rightarrow 5x = 150 \Rightarrow x = 30$. The angles are $2(30)+20 = 80^\circ$ and $3(30)+10 = 100^\circ$. The larger is $100^\circ$.

questions.question: In a figure, lines $p$ and $q$ are parallel. If an exterior angle created by a transversal measures $75^\circ$, what is the measure of the consecutive interior angle on the same side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle and the consecutive interior angle on the same side of the transversal are supplementary. Alternatively, the vertical angle to the $75^\circ$ exterior angle is also $75^\circ$ and lies inside the parallel lines. The consecutive interior angle to that is $180 - 75 = 105^\circ$.

questions.question: Two lines intersect to form four angles. If one angle is $(4x - 5)^\circ$ and the angle vertically opposite to it is $(3x + 15)^\circ$, what is the measure of an angle adjacent to these?
questions.explanation: Vertical angles are equal: $4x - 5 = 3x + 15 \Rightarrow x = 20$. The vertical angles are $4(20) - 5 = 75^\circ$. Adjacent angles on intersecting lines are supplementary: $180 - 75 = 105^\circ$.

questions.question: If line $L$ is perpendicular to line $M$, and line $M$ is parallel to line $N$, what is the relationship between line $L$ and line $N$?
questions.explanation: If a line is perpendicular to one of two parallel lines, it must also be perpendicular to the other. Since $L \perp M$ and $M \parallel N$, then $L \perp N$.

questions.question: In the figure, lines $s$ and $t$ are parallel and are intersected by transversal $k$. If the measure of one of the eight angles formed is $124^\circ$, which of the following could NOT be the measure of any of the other seven angles?
questions.explanation: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, only two unique angle measures are formed: the given angle ($124^\circ$) and its supplement ($180^\circ - 124^\circ = 56^\circ$). $66^\circ$ is not related to either of these values.

questions.question: Line $AB$ and line $CD$ intersect at point $E$. If the measure of $\angle AEC$ is $5x - 20$ and the measure of $\angle BED$ is $3x + 30$, what is the measure of $\angle AED$?
questions.explanation: $\angle AEC$ and $\angle BED$ are vertical angles, so they are equal: $5x - 20 = 3x + 30 \Rightarrow 2x = 50 \Rightarrow x = 25$. $\angle AEC = 5(25) - 20 = 105^\circ$. $\angle AED$ is supplementary to $\angle AEC$, so $\angle AED = 180 - 105 = 75^\circ$.

questions.question: Three lines intersect at a single point. If two of the angles formed are $45^\circ$ and $35^\circ$ and are adjacent to each other, what is the measure of the largest angle formed by any two of the three lines?
questions.explanation: The three lines create six angles around a point that sum to $360^\circ$. The given angles are $45^\circ$ and $35^\circ$. The third angle on that straight line is $180 - (45 + 35) = 100^\circ$. The vertical angles opposite them will also be $45^\circ$, $35^\circ$, and $100^\circ$. The largest individual angle is $100^\circ$.

questions.question: Lines $l$ and $m$ are parallel. Transversal $t$ intersects them such that the ratio of two supplementary angles is $2:3$. What is the measure of the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles sum to $180^\circ$. Let the angles be $2x$ and $3x$. $2x + 3x = 180 \Rightarrow 5x = 180 \Rightarrow x = 36$. The smaller angle is $2(36) = 72^\circ$.

questions.question: In a triangle, the measure of one angle is twice the measure of a second angle, and the third angle measures $60^\circ$. What is the measure of the largest angle in the triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is $180^\circ$. Let the angles be $x$, $2x$, and $60$. $x + 2x + 60 = 180 \Rightarrow 3x = 120 \Rightarrow x = 40$. The angles are $40^\circ$, $80^\circ$, and $60^\circ$. The largest is $80^\circ$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can confidently apply angle relationship theorems, solve for unknown variables in geometric figures, and identify parallel/perpendicular line properties.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on clearly distinguishing between vertical angles (equal) and supplementary angles (sum to 180), and remember that parallel lines cut by a transversal only produce two distinct angle measures.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: vertical angles are always equal, linear pairs are supplementary, and alternate interior angles are only equal when lines are parallel.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Start by labeling angle pairs on a sketch, write the corresponding equation (equal or sum to 180), and solve step-by-step. Visualizing the figure makes the relationships obvious.
